MINI MINUTES: Appearances: David Sauers for and with (executor) Jerome Sapiro Jr.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Nov-01-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: David Sauers for and with Jerome Sapiro Jr. (Executor).

Mr. Sauers stated that on December 16, 2002 the Executor was authorized to retain Hong Kong Counsel and pay his fees out of this Estate, that they agreed that Mr. Lee is not going to seek fees out of the Hong Kong proceeding if he is paid from this Estate, that the Hong Kong Estate is an ancillary proceeding and that all of the assets related to Hong Kong have been transferred here. Mr. Sauers also stated that all of the assets are to be distributed from this Estate, that $130,000.00 (U.S. dollars) remain in Hong Kong in a special interest bearing account, that it is more efficient to authorize Mr. Lee's payment out this Court, and that there will bo no issue of double payment or duplication of fees. With regard to the request to destroy tax preparation documents, Mr. Sauers stated that the retention guideline is six years, that the last year the Decedent had done any tax returns was in 2001, that the Executor gave written notice to all of the Decedent's Clients to retrieve their documents, that some Clients did and some did not, and that it has been eleven years. Mr. Sauers also explained services provided/performed to support his request for fees and suggested having the amended accounting on file by the end of this year. Mr. Sapiro asked to have the accounting period at the end of the calendar year, and a three year accounting period instead of five years. Cash distribution will be deferred until filing of the first account. The Court asked Mr. Sauers to provide an amended order leaving the amounts of fees blank. The Court granted the accounting petition (subject to receipt and review of a revised order).
Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy T. Bradley. (Not Reported)
